briefing.incidentSummary:
A zero-day vulnerability in a power grid SCADA system was exploited, causing a controlled blackout in an industrial zone for 6 hours.

The attacker didn't cause maximum damage — they demonstrated capability. A warning shot.

The exploit code was custom-built and vendor-specific. No patch existed. Only three people in the country had ever audited this SCADA system's codebase.
briefing.missionText:
Your Mission:

› Identify who wrote the zero-day exploit

› Determine if this was state-sponsored or rogue

› Trace the controlled nature of the attack — why stop early?

› Collect at least 4 correct evidences

› Flag the right suspect

suspects.name: Ronak S
suspects.profileNotes: One of only 3 researchers with SCADA codebase access. Discreetly sold the exploit to a foreign intelligence intermediary for $280,000 — but panicked and triggered the shutdown early, preventing full infrastructure damage. Financial trail leads to a Mauritius account.

suspects.name: Unknown Foreign Actor
suspects.profileNotes: Purchased the exploit — was the buyer. However the technical authorship of the zero-day and the decision to trigger it belong to Ronak S. The buyer only provided direction — Ronak executed.

suspects.name: Supriya K
suspects.profileNotes: Had remote access for scheduled maintenance but was on leave during the attack window. Access token was active but not used — confirms she did not execute the exploit.

suspects.name: Devraj M
suspects.profileNotes: Had warned about this vulnerability 2 years ago in an internal memo that was ignored. No current system access. His old memo makes him a witness, not a suspect.

items.label: Module ready. Tested on isolated clone. Confirm payment before I hand over the trigger.
items.detail: Recovered from a dark web escrow forum. Ronak S negotiated the exploit sale under the handle 'NullVector'. Linguistic analysis and PGP key confirm NullVector = Ronak S.

items.label: Abort. Shut it down now. This is going too far.
items.detail: Sent at 2:47 AM — 4 hours into the blackout, 2 hours before the buyer expected full escalation. Ronak S panicked and remotely triggered the shutdown sequence, cutting the attack short.

items.label: Recommend immediate patch of CVE-GRID-0091. Risk level: critical. This will be exploited.
items.detail: Devraj M warned about this exact vulnerability 2 years ago. His memo was classified and shelved. He is a witness to negligence, not a suspect.

items.label: zeroday_exploit_analysis.pdf
items.detail: Reverse-engineered exploit targets a buffer overflow in GridCore SCADA firmware v4.2.1 — a vulnerability that was never publicly disclosed. Code style analysis matches Ronak S's published research papers: identical comment formatting, variable naming, and error handling patterns.

items.label: mauritius_account_trace.pdf
items.detail: $280,000 transferred in 3 tranches to a Mauritius shell account. Account opened under a name matching Ronak S's alias used in academic conferences. Traced via SWIFT correspondent banking logs.

items.label: codebase_access_log.txt
items.detail: Of the 3 researchers with SCADA codebase access, only Ronak S accessed the firmware module containing the exploited buffer overflow — 14 times in the past 4 months. The other 2 researchers never accessed that specific module.

hiddenItem.label: nullvector_pgp_identity.txt
hiddenItem.detail: The PGP key used by forum handle 'NullVector' was cross-referenced against Ronak S's academic email signature. They share the same RSA 4096-bit key fingerprint — confirming Ronak S is NullVector.

minigame.title: Decode Forum Identity
minigame.hint: Caesar shift -3. What is the real name behind NullVector?
hints: It is a researcher's name followed by their initial.

onSuccess.message: nullvector_pgp_identity.txt is now available in your file feed.

items.label: Controlled Shutdown Analysis
items.detail: The blackout was terminated via a shutdown command sent over the same channel as the exploit — remotely, from the attacker's side. This was not a defensive response by the grid operators. The attacker chose to stop. This suggests remorse or fear — not state-level discipline.

items.label: Code Authorship Analysis
items.detail: Automated stylometry analysis of the exploit code against 12 published SCADA security papers returns a 91.3% match to Ronak S's writing style. Second-highest match is 41%.

items.label: Jump Server Trace
items.detail: Attack was routed through a custom VPN terminating at IP 45.61.185.22 — a jump server in Singapore. Server was rented using a credit card in Ronak S's name at a past conference hotel.

timeline.title: Vulnerability Identified — Ignored
timeline.description: Devraj M files memo about CVE-GRID-0091. Shelved.

timeline.title: Codebase Access Pattern
timeline.description: Ronak S accesses buffer overflow module 14 times

timeline.title: Dark Web Negotiation
timeline.description: NullVector (Ronak S) auctions exploit on dark web forum

timeline.title: Payment Received
timeline.description: $280,000 in 3 tranches to Mauritius shell account

timeline.title: Exploit Triggered
timeline.description: Zero-day executed on GridCore SCADA system

timeline.title: Blackout Begins
timeline.description: Industrial zone loses power — 6 facilities offline

timeline.title: Attacker Self-Terminates
timeline.description: Ronak S sends abort command — panic shutdown

perfect.title: Case Solved!
perfect.subtitle: Ronak S exposed as the zero-day seller who panicked mid-attack.
perfect.label: CASE CLOSED

partial.title: Insufficient Evidence
partial.subtitle: Correct suspect but the chain is incomplete.
partial.label: PARTIAL

wrongAccusation.title: Wrong Suspect
wrongAccusation.subtitle: The real culprit was Ronak S.
wrongAccusation.label: CASE FAILED

aria.welcomeToHub:
...A blackout. 6 hours. A warning shot — not a killing blow.
Whoever did this could have gone further. They chose not to.
That tells you something. Find out who flinched.
aria.exploreFeeds:
Exploit code. Financial transfers. Dark web traces.
This was a researcher who crossed a line — and then tried to step back.
aria.viewEvidence:
The code style. The codebase access. The controlled shutdown.
This was personal knowledge, not just technical skill.
aria.markEvidence: Good. The evidence is forming a portrait.
aria.decryptionHint: The forum identity is encoded. Decode it — the PGP key will confirm everything.
aria.flagSuspect:
A trace is forming.
You're on to something — keep pulling the thread.
More fragments remain. Don't stop now.
